questions.prompt: A small drone is launched from a platform 1 meter above the ground. Its height is modeled by $f(x)=1+x^2$, where $x$ is time in seconds and $x\ge 0$. What are the domain and range?

llmContext.overviewText: Use context for domain (time cannot be negative), then find minimum output from the smallest allowed input.

nodes.title: Find allowed inputs
nodes.reference: Because x is time, x>=0. Squaring adds no extra restriction.

nodes.title: Find minimum output
nodes.reference: f(0)=1 is the minimum; x^2 then increases, so outputs are >=1.

llmContext.overviewText: Square-root expressions require the radicand to be nonnegative.

questions.prompt: Determine whether $S(x)=x^{-5}$ is even, odd, or neither.

llmContext.overviewText: Test parity by comparing S(-x) with S(x) and -S(x).

questions.prompt: Determine whether $h(t)=|t^3|$ is even, odd, or neither.

llmContext.overviewText: Absolute value can remove sign changes from odd powers.
Composition applies inside-out: h first, then g, then f.

questions.prompt: Given $f(x)=\frac{x}{x-2}$, find a function $g(x)$ such that $f(g(x))=x$.

llmContext.overviewText: Set g/(g-2)=x and solve algebraically for g in terms of x.

questions.prompt: Use a trig identity to simplify $\cos(x-\pi/2)$.

llmContext.overviewText: Use cosine subtraction formula and unit-circle values at pi/2.

nodes.title: Expand and substitute
nodes.reference: cos(x-pi/2)=cos x cos(pi/2)+sin x sin(pi/2)=0+sin x.

questions.prompt: Find all $u$ in $[0,2\pi]$ such that $\sin^2 u=3/4$.

llmContext.overviewText: Take square roots to get sin u = +/-sqrt(3)/2 and use the unit circle.

nodes.title: Solve trig values
nodes.reference: Positive in Q1/Q2 and negative in Q3/Q4.

questions.prompt: A trail elevation model is piecewise: for $-4\le x\le 0$, $y=\sqrt{-x}$; for $0<x\le 4$, $y=\sqrt{x}$. What are the overall domain and range?

llmContext.overviewText: Analyze each piece on its interval, then combine domain/range carefully using endpoint inclusion.

nodes.title: Left branch
nodes.reference: On [-4,0], sqrt(-x) runs from 2 down to 0, so range [0,2].

nodes.title: Right branch
nodes.reference: On (0,4], sqrt(x) runs from just above 0 up to 2, so range (0,2].

llmContext.overviewText: Use inverse relationship between exp and natural log, then account for the negative exponent.
